description:
Compare Best Health Plans with Ditto: https://ditto.sh/6zwj4x 

After the GST reforms, many of you asked us to bring back our annual insurance recommendation video early, so we’re back, starting with the Best Health Insurance Plans for 2026. 

In this video, we reveal the Best Health Insurance Plans for 2026, shortlisted through a transparent and data-backed process from 31 insurers across India. It’s a carefully researched framework designed to help you protect your family without falling for sales traps or half-baked advice.

Here’s how we selected the top insurers:

1. Claim Settlement Ratio (CSR)
2. Incurred Claims Ratio (ICR)
3. Complaints Ratio
4. Network Hospitals

After applying these filters, we arrived at our Top 3 Health Insurance Plans for 2026, each tailored for a different kind of policyholder. Whether you’re buying your first policy, managing chronic conditions, or looking for a simple digital plan, this video will help you decide confidently.

comments.text: @pranjalkamra Hello Sir, thanks for providing us such unbiased reviews. But this approach of elimination of insurance companies might not be right as some eliminated companies might have other better features which are not compared at the later stages. For example, if you are eliminating the companies on the basis of claim ratio first and then you are comparing the policies on the basis of number of available cashless hospitals, you might have eliminated some companies which have even better number of cashless hospitals than the ones you are comparing. Also when you are comparing claim ratio of a company, some companies have lower claim ratio only because the number of legitimate claim are less. So in my opinion eliminating a company based on a single feature without considering its other feature is not a proper approach. It might be possible that a company having only lower claim ratio but has all other features better than other companies, might get eliminated only because one negative feature and that also because the number of legitimate claims that were made was less. I think you understood my point.

comments.text:
say no to Niva Bupa based on my experiance
I want to share my real experience with Niva Bupa and Policy Bazaar. My policy was expiring on 4 Sept 2025. Policy Bazaar called and offered an upgrade plan, so I paid Rs 18847 through them. After a few days, Niva Bupa rejected the upgrade, so I requested a refund. Even after waiting 20 days, no refund came.

Meanwhile, to avoid a lapse, I renewed my existing policy directly on the Niva Bupa website by paying Rs 17463. The new policy was issued successfully. But suddenly, on 16 Sept, I received a mail saying my policy was being cancelled. I immediately replied that I never requested any cancellation and only wanted refund of the extra amount. Still, Niva Bupa cancelled my valid renewed policy and later refunded both payments.

Now they are saying the cancellation request came from Policy Bazaar, but my renewed policy was direct, not through Policy Bazaar. Even the policy document clearly shows intermediary as Directweb Niva Bupa, not Policy Bazaar. Despite many mails, calls, and complaint to IRDAI Bima Bharosa, there is no proper action or reinstatement.

My policy had just completed 3 years waiting period and was eligible for full benefits, yet they terminated it wrongly. It feels like intentional cancellation and complete negligence. Both Niva Bupa and Policy Bazaar are passing blame to each other, but the customer suffers.

Please be very careful while renewing or upgrading with Niva Bupa or Policy Bazaar. Their coordination, transparency, and support after payment are very poor.

comments.text:
Wow, did not expected this from pranjal Kamara .
Check irdai data,  about 100 years of experience and less complaints,,psu are always better.
Their primary goal is customer service।।whereas private players open company just for profit.
Still why doesn't any influencer promote PSUs
PSUs in the insurance sector demonstrate superior stability and customer trust. For approximately 100 years, their Claim Settlement Ratio has consistently remained above 90%. Furthermore, customer complaints are exceptionally low, averaging below 10 per 10,000 users.
The core difference in market strategy lies in agent compensation and regulation.
The Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India (IRDAI) sets strict commission limits for agents that companies must follow. PSUs adhere rigidly to these rules, a compliance which is confirmed by two mandatory annual audits.
Private insurers, facing fewer restrictions, often circumvent these limits to pay agents much higher, "handsome" commissions, sometimes outside of regular business channels. Consequently, commission-driven agents exclusively promote private insurance companies.
While PSUs may have a slightly smaller network of empanelled hospitals, their better reputation and long-standing superior CSR provide a significant advantage over private players.

comments.text:
🍳 Here are 10 different types of egg omelettes, with a description of their taste, flavor profile, and a brief recipe for each:
1. Classic French Omelette
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: The French omelette is a masterpiece of simplicity. It's known for its smooth, pale yellow exterior with no browning, and a moist, tender, and custardy interior. The flavor is delicate, primarily that of rich butter and fresh eggs, often enhanced with fines herbes (parsley, chives, tarragon, chervil) for a light, herbal lift.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Whisk 2-3 eggs vigorously with salt and pepper (and sometimes a dash of water/cream) until just homogenous.
   * Melt a generous knob of butter in an 8-inch nonstick pan over medium-high heat until foamy.
   * Pour in the eggs and immediately shake and swirl the pan while stirring the eggs with a fork in rapid figure-eights for about 10 seconds.
   * Stop stirring, let the surface set slightly, add herbs/filling (if any) down the middle, and gently roll/fold the omelette onto a plate, forming an oval shape.
2. Spanish Tortilla (Tortilla Española)
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: This is more of a thick, dense potato and egg cake than a typical omelette. It's hearty, savory, and robust. The flavor is dominated by the slowly caramelized onions and tender, pan-fried potatoes, bound together by the moist egg. It's deeply satisfying and often served at room temperature.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Thinly slice potatoes and onions. Gently fry them in a generous amount of olive oil until very soft and tender, but not browned.
   * Drain the oil, reserving some, and mix the potatoes and onions with 6-8 beaten eggs seasoned with salt.
   * Heat a tablespoon of the reserved oil in a nonstick pan. Pour in the egg/potato mixture and cook on low-medium heat until the bottom is set.
   * Invert the tortilla onto a plate, then slide it back into the pan to cook the other side until firm and golden brown.
3. American Diner Omelette
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: The American style is a hearty, folded omelette that is typically fully cooked and often lightly browned. It is known for its generous, customizable fillings, resulting in a rich, savory, and often cheesy flavor profile. Fillings might include ham, cheese (Cheddar, Monterey Jack), bell peppers, and onions, giving it a bold, multi-textured taste.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Sauté any vegetables or meats first (e.g., diced onion, bell pepper, ham). Set aside.
   * Whisk 3 eggs with a splash of water/milk, salt, and pepper.
   * Melt butter in a nonstick pan over medium heat. Pour in the eggs and cook without stirring until the edges start to set.
   * Gently push the cooked edges towards the center, tilting the pan to allow the uncooked egg to flow underneath.
   * When the top is mostly set but still slightly moist, add the fillings and cheese to one half.
   * Fold the unfilled half over the filling and cook briefly until the cheese is melted.
4. Japanese Tamagoyaki (Rolled Omelette)
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: Tamagoyaki is a sweet or savory layered, rolled egg dish. It has a distinctive firm yet springy texture. The classic flavor is sweet and umami, thanks to additions like dashi (broth), mirin (sweet rice wine), and soy sauce. It is moist, delicate, and often served as a side dish or in sushi.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Whisk 3-4 eggs with dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and sugar/salt. Strain the mixture for smoothness.
   * Lightly oil a rectangular tamagoyaki pan and heat over medium-low.
   * Pour in a thin layer of the egg mixture. As it sets, roll it tightly to one end of the pan.
   * Oil the empty space, pour in another thin layer of egg, making sure it connects with the cooked roll.
   * Roll the cooked egg back over the new layer. Repeat this process several times until all the egg mixture is used, creating a multi-layered roll.
5. Frittata
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: Though technically an Italian baked egg dish, it’s a cousin to the omelette.  It is thick, open-faced, and dense, with fillings mixed throughout the egg mixture rather than folded inside. The flavor is rich and rustic, often including vegetables like spinach or zucchini, cheese (like Parmesan or ricotta), and herbs, giving it a strong savory profile.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Sauté fillings (e.g., onions, spinach, sausage) in an oven-safe, nonstick skillet.
   * Whisk 6-8 eggs with milk/cream, salt, pepper, and cheese. Pour the mixture over the fillings in the pan.
   * Cook on the stovetop until the edges are set (about 5 minutes).
   * Transfer the skillet to a preheated oven to finish cooking the top until the frittata is puffed and set.
6. Indian Masala Omelette
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: This omelette is highly flavorful and spicy! It’s thin, cooked through, and heavily seasoned with South Asian spices and fresh aromatics. The taste is bold, savory, and zesty, with the crunch of finely diced onions, green chilies, cilantro, and sometimes ginger/garlic paste, all spiced with chili powder, turmeric, and cumin.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Finely dice onions, green chilies, and fresh coriander.
   * Whisk 2-3 eggs with the diced aromatics, salt, a pinch of turmeric, and chili powder/garam masala.
   * Heat oil or butter in a pan over medium heat.
   * Pour in the egg mixture and cook until set and lightly browned on both sides. Fold or serve flat.
7. Denver/Western Omelette
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: Similar to the American Diner style but with a strict set of classic fillings. It's savory, smoky, and textural. The definitive flavor comes from diced ham, onions, and green bell peppers. It is a robust and hearty omelette, giving a pleasant salty, smoky, and slightly sweet-peppery taste.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Sauté diced ham, green bell peppers, and onions until softened.
   * Whisk 3 eggs with a splash of milk/water, salt, and pepper.
   * Pour eggs into a buttered nonstick pan over medium heat. Cook until the edges set.
   * Add the ham/pepper/onion mix and cheese (optional) over one side.
   * Fold into a neat half-moon shape.
8. Soufflé Omelette (Puffy Omelette)
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: This is a spectacular, extremely light, and airy omelette, almost cloud-like. It is delicate and has a very mild, almost sweet egg flavor, often enhanced with a simple cheese filling or powdered sugar dusting. The texture is the star—it’s tender and spongy like a soufflé, achieved by separating and whipping the egg whites.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Separate egg yolks and whites. Whisk the yolks with a little salt.
   * Whip the egg whites to stiff peaks.
   * Gently fold the yolks into the whites.
   * Melt butter in a nonstick pan over low heat. Pour in the mixture.
   * Cook on the stovetop until the bottom is set, then finish in a hot oven until puffed and golden brown. Fold gently before serving. 
9. Omurice (Japanese Omelette Rice)
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: Omurice (Omelette and Rice) is a hybrid comfort food where a thin, plain omelette is wrapped around or placed on a mound of fried rice. The flavor is primarily savory and tangy, with the chicken-and-vegetable fried rice seasoned with ketchup or a demi-glace sauce. The egg acts as a delicate, buttery covering.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Make "Chicken Rice" (fried rice with chicken, peas, carrots, and ketchup). Form into a mound.
   * Whisk 2 eggs with a pinch of salt. Cook in a well-oiled pan until a thin layer is just set and still slightly soft.
   * Place the rice mound onto the center of the egg.
   * Fold the sides of the omelette over the rice, or gently flip the whole assembly onto a plate, covering the rice. Top with a drizzle of ketchup.
10. Greek Omelette
 * Description, Taste, and Flavor: A robust, Mediterranean-flavored omelette that is highly savory, salty, and herb-forward. The key flavor notes are the tangy, salty crumbles of feta cheese, fresh flavors of oregano and parsley, and the slightly sweet earthiness of tomatoes and  spinach (often pre-sautéed). It offers a bright, rustic taste.
 * Brief Recipe:
   * Sauté spinach, diced tomato, and a pinch of oregano in olive oil until softened.
   * Whisk 3 eggs with salt, pepper, and finely chopped parsley.
   * Pour eggs into the pan over the vegetables. Scatter generous crumbles of feta cheese on top.
   * Cook on medium-low until set. Fold or serve open-faced.
